creator: swills@FreeBSD.org
text:
On 07/08/12 12:10, Mikhail Tsatsenko wrote:
> The following reply was made to PR ports/168727; it has been noted by GNATS.
> 
> From: Mikhail Tsatsenko <m.tsatsenko@gmail.com>
> To: bug-followup@FreeBSD.org
> Cc:  
> Subject: Re: ports/168727: [PATCH] www/redmine: update and UNBREAK
> Date: Sun, 8 Jul 2012 20:01:04 +0400
> 
>  A newer version is available.
>  http://tsatsenko.ru/files/redmine-2.0.3.patch

I took a look at the previous version and I'm not quite happy with the
way it was done. It bundles all the gems in a different directory and I
think things won't work correctly with different versions of ruby. Plus,
that's against how we've done things in ports. Seems like it should use
the gems from ports. If it can't work with that, we should find another
solution such as having multiple versions of gems installed at the same
time from ports. If this new version is done the same way, I think the
same would apply.

Thanks,
Steve
